items.text: Class Q grants planning permission for a change of use, from certain agricultural buildings — including some former agricultural buildings, and the land within their curtilage — to Class C3 dwellinghouses. It covers the building operations that are reasonably necessary to convert the building. And since the 21st of May 2024, it covers limited, single-storey rear extensions too. Now, the right exists for a simple reason. It's there to release redundant farm buildings for housing, without testing the merits of living in the countryside against the development plan. But it's also the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of the Part 3 rights. It picks up far more than its share of prior approval appeals. And it rewards forensic preparation, more than any other permitted development right. Because eligibility turns on history, fact, and structure — and those have to be evidenced, not asserted.

Class Q grants planning permission for a change of use, from certain agricultural buildings — including some former agricultural buildings, and the land within their curtilage — to Class C3 dwellinghouses. It covers the building operations that are reasonably necessary to convert the building. And since the 21st of May 2024, it covers limited, single-storey rear extensions too. Now, the right exists for a simple reason. It's there to release redundant farm buildings for housing, without testing the merits of living in the countryside against the development plan. But it's also the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of the Part 3 rights. It picks up far more than its share of prior approval appeals. And it rewards forensic preparation, more than any other permitted development right. Because eligibility turns on history, fact, and structure — and those have to be evidenced, not asserted.
